tagline: AI pair programming in your terminal
subheadline: Aider lets you pair program with LLMs to start a new project or build on your existing codebase.
value_proposition: A terminal-based AI pair programming tool that integrates with leading LLMs to help developers write, edit, and manage code across any project.
target_audience: Software developers and engineers who prefer terminal-based workflows and want AI assistance integrated directly into their coding environment.

testimonials: I like aider :) — Chenwei Cui on X
Aider is also my best friend. — jzn21 on Reddit
Aider is hands down the best. And it's free and opensource. — AriyaSavakaLurker on Reddit
Aider is amazing, coupled with Sonnet 3.5 it's quite mind blowing. — Josh Dingus on Discord
Aider ... has easily quadrupled my coding productivity. — SOLAR_FIELDS on Hacker News
Try aider! One of the best ways to vibe code. — Chris Wall on X

pricing.price_anchoring: No pricing mentioned; positioned as free and open-source, with costs only being LLM API key usage from third-party providers.

features: Works with cloud and local LLMs including Claude, DeepSeek, OpenAI models
Maps entire codebase to improve context in large projects
Automatic git commits with sensible commit messages
IDE integration — works from within favorite editors
Voice-to-code: speak requests and have aider implement changes
Automatic linting and test execution after each change
differentiators: Codebase mapping that helps it work well in larger projects
Works with almost any LLM including local models
Automatic git integration with commit messages
trust_signals: Open source with public GitHub repository

design_patterns.layout: Single-column hero with stats bar, feature grid below, testimonials section, getting started code block, and footer links
design_patterns.color_scheme: Not determinable from text alone; likely dark/neutral terminal-inspired palette given the CLI product
design_patterns.typography: Clean, functional — technical product with code snippets displayed prominently
design_patterns.visual_style: Technical/minimal with embedded video demo in hero
notable_patterns: Embedded video demo in hero section
Live stats bar with emoji icons (stars, installs, tokens)
Code snippet getting-started section with syntax highlighting

emotional_tone: Confident and community-driven, with a casual developer-friendly voice; aspirational around productivity gains.
key_objections_addressed: Works with existing codebases, not just new projects
Supports local LLMs for users concerned about sending code to cloud APIs
Works with nearly any LLM — not locked into one provider
IDE integration addresses concern about leaving existing editor workflow
Git integration addresses fear of losing or mismanaging AI-generated changes
missing_or_weak: No company logos or enterprise social proof
No explicit pricing or cost guidance beyond needing an API key
No security or data privacy messaging for enterprise or privacy-conscious users
Testimonials are all from social media/Reddit — no named professionals or companies
No case studies or deeper before/after productivity stories
'Singularity 88%' metric is unexplained and likely confusing to new visitors
No clear FAQ section addressing common hesitations
No email capture or newsletter CTA for visitors not ready to install
standout_elements: Quantified social proof metrics (stars, installs, tokens/week) displayed prominently in hero
Video demo embedded directly in hero without requiring a click-through
Voice-to-code is a genuinely differentiated feature rarely highlighted by competitors
Getting started section uses real terminal commands — reduces friction for technical audience
Open source positioning removes pricing as a barrier entirely
Breadth of LLM support (cloud and local) is a meaningful differentiator clearly called out
